hypothesis: If the fragment-based generation of linear polyamines, refined force fields, a two-stage MD-GCMC evaluation, and Bayesian optimization with Thompson sampling across multiple polymer representations are used to search for high-CO2-adsorbing polyamines, then the framework will identify candidates with higher CO2 adsorption capacity at 0.4 mbar than randomly sampled candidates, because Bayesian optimization efficiently learns structure–property relationships from simulation data and concentrates the search on the most promising regions of chemical space.
